Question: What is the combined total yearly microbial respiration due to decomposition across these four peatland settings: the perched hollow central site experiencing periodic flooding from river backflows with iron-associated organic particle transport and slow-decaying dwarf shrub litter; the rewetted farm peatland with a persistent moisture fringed zone supporting methane-producing microbes, where organic matter seeps into intertwined stream channels from banks and woody plants are in a moderate stage of decay releasing lignin-rich detritus; and finally, at the coastal peat tongue, where a long water retention period under low evaporation feeds two different organic carbon transport pathways—one through underground fen transition water flow associated with a persistent sedge mat low in nitrogen, and the other via surface runoff through Sphagnum-based litter allowing fen sedge with irregular senescence waves?

Question: What is the total greenhouse gas flux recorded across all data points that include (1) the transitional fen zone with vascular grasses in Forsinard Flows, Scotland measured during the 2018 monthly automated chambers, where methane moves through plant tissues, (2) the partially drained and re-wetted peat with early grass succession at Mer Bleue Bog, Canada, also from the 2018 monthly automated chambers, where carbon release mainly comes from aerobic surface decomposition, (3) the raised hummock area dominated by Sphagnum moss and dwarf shrubs at Forsinard measured in the 2021 multi-season campaign showing nitrous oxide emissions linked to denitrification during drying periods, and (4) the peat plateau zone with oxygen-exposed surface and root layers in the Sundarbans mangrove transect, Bangladesh, sampled in 2023 with day and night contrasts, where methane uptake is driven by oxidation at aerobic lawns?

Question: Considering the combined annual carbon burial in sediments, what is the total estimated amount for (1) mangrove-dominated estuarine communities with extensive pneumatophore root systems in the Ganges-Brahmaputra delta and Sundarbans connected to the Bay of Bengal embayment restoration project, (2) oyster reef ecosystems that enhance sediment retention within the Patagonian inner fjords associated with the Mediterranean saltmarsh living shoreline program, (3) coastal peat fringes undergoing gradual wetland peat formation in shallow Baltic Sea embayments as part of the Gulf of Guinea coastline stabilization efforts, and (4) seagrass meadows exhibiting rhizome peat development on the Bay of Bengal coastal plain affected by monsoonal river sediment flows also under the Mediterranean saltmarsh restoration framework?

Question: What is the total (sum) CarbonFluxDensity for these four specific records: the measurement at TR-OD-06 (Okavango fringe 18.30S, 23.25E) from the "2023 protocol harmonization workshop campaign — European peatland observatory network" with HydrogeomorphicClass "bog-pool complex with bogbean and sedge mats"; the measurement at TR-FC-01 (Flow Country 58.55N, -3.40W) from the "Mid-2022 saline intrusion observational campaign — Sundarbans estuarine peatlands, Bangladesh" with HydrogeomorphicClass "ombrotrophic dome (raised bog) with hummock-hollow microtopography"; the measurement at TR-LX-08 (Loxahatchee NWR 26.90N, -80.25W) from the "2020 fen restoration baseline survey — Kalmthout Heath, Belgium" with HydrogeomorphicClass "string-flark patterned fen with lateral sedge strings"; and the measurement at TR-LX-08 (Loxahatchee NWR 26.90N, -80.25W) from the "Autumn 2022 chamber-fence repeat survey — Moor House, UK" with HydrogeomorphicClass "ombrotrophic dome (raised bog) with hummock-hollow microtopography"?
SQL Query: SELECT SUM(CarbonFluxDensity) AS CarbonFluxDensity FROM (SELECT CarbonFluxDensity AS CarbonFluxDensity FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE MonitoringCampaign = "2023 protocol harmonization workshop campaign — European peatland observatory network" AND HydrogeomorphicClass = "bog-pool complex with bogbean and sedge mats" AND TransectReference = "TR-OD-06 (Okavango fringe 18.30S, 23.25E)" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonFluxDensity AS CarbonFluxDensity FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE MonitoringCampaign = "Mid-2022 saline intrusion observational campaign — Sundarbans estuarine peatlands, Bangladesh" AND HydrogeomorphicClass = "ombrotrophic dome (raised bog) with hummock-hollow microtopography" AND TransectReference = "TR-FC-01 (Flow Country 58.55N, -3.40W)" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonFluxDensity AS CarbonFluxDensity FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE MonitoringCampaign = "2020 fen restoration baseline survey — Kalmthout Heath, Belgium" AND HydrogeomorphicClass = "string-flark patterned fen with lateral sedge strings" AND TransectReference = "TR-LX-08 (Loxahatchee NWR 26.90N, -80.25W)" UNION ALL SELECT CarbonFluxDensity AS CarbonFluxDensity FROM PeatlandFluxInventory WHERE MonitoringCampaign = "Autumn 2022 chamber-fence repeat survey — Moor House, UK" AND HydrogeomorphicClass = "ombrotrophic dome (raised bog) with hummock-hollow microtopography" AND TransectReference = "TR-LX-08 (Loxahatchee NWR 26.90N, -80.25W)");

Question: What is the combined total carbon dioxide release rate, in g CO2 m-2 day-1, obtained by summing the FluxMagnitude values recorded at (1) the EDC-MX07 montane grassland flux mast station for the seasonally inundated floodplain willow carr with hydric soils where canopy respiration diurnal hysteresis was measured by branch chambers during the instrument intercomparison 2020 cross-protocol round-robin; (2) the EDC-PH03 temperate peatland micrometeorological node array for the climax old-growth conifer stand with deep organic horizon where lateral dissolved organic carbon export acted as a fluvial CO2 source during the summer 2019 isotope-tracer pulse-chase deployment; (3) the EDC-AR11 arctic polygon tundra eddy station cluster for the aggrading pasture-to-bog transition with Sphagnum co-dominance where sediment porewater CO2 release was measured during tidal exposure cycles in the instrument intercomparison 2020 cross-protocol round-robin; and (4) the EDC-PH03 temperate peatland micrometeorological node array for the heathland with ericaceous dwarf-shrub dominance and thin peat where soil surface diffusive CO2 efflux was measured via automated chamber array during the growing-season 2022 high-frequency eddy covariance campaign?
